sanskrit:
कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि।
योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये॥
transliteration:
kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair api
yoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgaṁ tyaktvātma-śuddhaye
literal_meaning: With body, with mind, with intelligence, even with the senses alone—yogis perform action, abandoning attachment for the purification of the self.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that true renunciation is internal: engage every faculty (body, mind, intellect, senses) without clinging, and the very act of detached engagement becomes a self-purifying therapy.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth mindset—willing to re-engage life fully while practicing non-attachment to outcomes.
trigger_scenario: Returning to work after burnout: you use the same laptop, meetings, and deadlines, but now as tools for service rather than self-worth, noticing stress dissolve.
anchor_text: Use every part of yourself without clinging, and the doing itself cleanses you.
